title: DotGlobeHero Component
content:
The `DotGlobeHero` component is a React component built with Next.js and React Three Fiber to display an animated, wireframe globe as a background for hero sections. ## Purpose This component provides a visually engaging, interactive globe animation that can serve as a dynamic background for landing pages or hero sections. It leverages `react-three-fiber` to render a 3D globe within a React application. ## Key Exports ### `DotGlobeHero` Component A React component that renders a full-screen container with a 3D globe animation. It accepts children that will be rendered in the foreground, overlaid on the globe. #### Props * `rotationSpeed` (number, optional): Controls the speed of the globe's rotation. Defaults to `0.005`. * `globeRadius` (number, optional): Determines the radius of the globe. Defaults to `1`. * `className` (string, optional): Allows for custom styling of the main container element. * `children` (React.ReactNode, optional): Content to be displayed on top of the globe animation. #### Ref This component forwards a ref to its main `div` element, allowing external manipulation or access. ## Usage Example ```tsx import { DotGlobeHero } from '@/components/ui/globe-hero'; const HomePage = () => { return (

Welcome to DocMed

Your comprehensive documentation platform.

 ); }; export default HomePage; ``` ## Internal Components ### `Globe` Component An internal component responsible for rendering the 3D globe mesh using `react-three-fiber`. It handles the geometry, materials, and animation of the globe. #### Props * `rotationSpeed` (number): The speed at which the globe rotates. * `radius` (number): The radius of the globe. This component uses `useRef` to get a reference to the `THREE.Group` and `useFrame` to update its rotation on each frame, creating the animation. ## Dependencies * `react` * `@react-three/fiber` * `@react-three/drei` * `three` * `@/lib/utils` (for `cn` utility)
